Fern Polrrrcal PnecrrcEs CoMMrssroN
428 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento. CA 95g14_2-129
(916) 322-5660 0 Fax (916) 322_0886
April9, 2008
Richard A. McDonald
Jeffer. Mangels, Butler & Marmaro,LLp
1900 Avenue of the Stars, 7th Floor
Los Angeles, Californi a 90067 -4308
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-08-034
Dear Mr. McDonald:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") and is based on the facts present.d.i
The Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act ai a finder of
fact when it renders advice. (ln re oglesby (r975) 1 Fppc ops. 71.) Additionally, we
base our advice solely on the provisions of the Act and do not address the applicability, if
any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interesi or
Government Code Section 1090. Because we lack specific information identifying the
particulars of factual transactions, we offer informal advice. Informal assistance does not
confer the immunity provided by a Commission opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83 I 14, Regulation lB3Zg.)
Question
May you, a member of the city of pasadena planning commission, in your
private capacity as an attorney, appear on behalf of a client in front of City of pa.adena
commissions or agencies other than the planning commission?
Conclusion
Although you may not appear in front of the planning commission, yoll may
represent clients before other agencies and boards under thelollowing conditions:
' The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections g1000 through 9l0lzt. All
stattltoryret'erencesaretotheGovernmentCode.unlessotherwiseindicated. TheregulatiorisgftheFair
Ptrlitical Practices Commission are contained in Sections ltt I l0 through lgggT of Title 2 0f the califbrnia
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2. Divisioi 6 of the Califirrnia C.de .f
Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.
;in


File No. I-08-034
Page No. 2
You are a member of the City of Pasadena Planning Commission. In your private
capacity, you are employed as an attorney. As a private atiorney, you have been retained
to represent a client on a development project. The project will have to be approved by
various city commissions, including, but not limitedto, the planning com*isrion. you
understand that you are likely to have a conflict of interest urnder thI ect if you represent
a client before the planning commission. Your question concerns the extent to which this
rule applies to your appearance before other City of Pasadena commissions or agencies.
Analysis
Section 8710O prohibits any public official from making, parricipating in making,
or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental deiision in
which the official has a financial interest. A public official lias a "financial interest,, in a
governmental decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that
the governmental decision will have a material financial effect on one o, n,or" of the
public official's economic interests. (Section g7103; Regulation 1g700(a).)
The Commission has adopted a standard eighrstep analysis for deciding whether
an official has a disqualifying conflict of interest. (negulation igzoo (bXl) - fs'l.l Since
your question concerns only the issue of making, parti-ipating, or influencing a decision.
we limit our discussion to this part of the standard analysis. Fo. pr.pores ofihis
discussion the following definitions apply:
o d public official makes a govemmental decision "when he or she votes on a matter,
obligates the agency to a course of action, or enters into any contractual agreement on
behalf of the agency. (Regulation l87OZ.l.)
a. The agency you appear before is neither under the budgetary nor appointive
control of your agency; and
b. You do not act or purport to act in an official capacity as a member of your
agency.
Facts
An official "participates in making a governmental decision" when he or she
negotiates, advises or makes recommendations to the decisionmaker. (Regulation
18702.2.)
o An official "attempts to use his or her official position to influence a decision,, if ,.the
official contacts, or appears before, or otherwiie attempts to influence any member,
officer, employee or consurtant of the agency." (Reguiation 1s702.3.)
The first two of these definitions, "making" a government decision and
"participating" in a government decision, generally "r"o*pus situations when the
official is acting in his or her official capacity. Your quest^ion implicates the third
a


File No. I-08-034
Page No.3
definition, concerning when an official is using his or her official position to influence a
govemment decision' There are two situations that are considered^ using one,s official
position to influence a governmental decision. The first is when o gor"--*ental decision
is within or before the public official's own agency or an agency appointed by or subject
to the budgetary control of the public official s ogin.y. Regutaiion 1g702.3(a) srates that
an official "is attempting to use his or her officiai position to influence the decision if, for
the purpose of influencing the decision, the official contacts, or appears before, or
otherwise attempts to influence, any member, officer, employee or consultant of the
agency' Attempts to influence include, but are not limitea to, appearances or contacts by
the official on behalf of a business entity, client, or cllstomer." As you noted in your
letter,. this would prevent you from representing clients before the pianning commission
:1::lll ig"ncy, if any, appointed by or subjeci to budgetary contr-ol of thJ planning
commlsslon.'
The second situation is when the govemment decision is before an agency that is
not the official's own agency or appointed by or subject to his or her o*n ug""n"y,.
budgetary control. Under this situation, Regulatio, igzoz.:(b) states that ai .,official is
attempting to use his or her official position to influence the decision if, for the purpose
of influencing the decision, the official acts or purports to act on behaliof, o, u, tt "
representative of, his orher agency to any membei, officer, employee or consultant of an
agency." This would allow you to represent a client in front of tnese other agencies, so
long I you do not purport to act as a representative of the planning commission. This
prohibition from acting as a representative of the planning commission includes, but is
not limited to, using agency letterhead in any communicaiion or using your agency staff
to contact members of other agencies.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me
at (916) 332-5660.
Sincerely,
By
Pamela
Legal Intem, Legal Division
PA:jgl
- We are assuming for purposes of your question that any client you represent befirre the planning
commissionisasourceof incometoyou(seeseciion^g7r03(crandRegulation ru703.3)andis..directly,.
i'.",olYd in the planning commission's decision (see Section 87103 aniRegularions lg704.l(a) and
18705.3(a)).
